example_prompt: Create a Flutterwave v4 transfer to a pre-registered recipient using a previously created sender_id and recipient_id.

action.description: Execution mode. "instant" disburses immediately. "deferred" creates the transfer but holds disbursement until explicitly initiated via PUT /transfers/{id} with initiate=true. "scheduled" disburses at the date_time provided in disburse_option.

payment_instruction.description: The transfer instruction referencing pre-created sender and recipient resources.

recipient_id.description: ID of a recipient pre-created via POST /transfers/recipients.

source_currency.description: ISO 4217 source currency code (the currency debited from the merchant's wallet).

applies_to.description: Whether `value` is denominated in the destination_currency or source_currency. The other side is computed via Flutterwave's FX rate.

sender_id.description: Optional. ID of a sender pre-created via POST /transfers/senders. Required for cross-border corridors that demand sender KYC (USD/GBP/EUR/EGP).

reference.description: Optional merchant-supplied unique reference. Alphanumeric and hyphens, 6-42 chars. Must be unique — reusing returns 409.

narration.description: Optional narration shown to the recipient where supported by the rail.

disburse_option.description: Required when action="scheduled". Defines when the transfer is released.

date_time.description: Scheduled disbursement date/time in 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' (24-hour) format.

timezone.description: IANA timezone, e.g. UTC, Africa/Lagos, America/New_York, Asia/Tokyo, Europe/London.

callback_url.description: Webhook URL Flutterwave POSTs to when the transfer status changes. Overrides the merchant default webhook URL.

meta.description: Arbitrary key/value metadata stored on the transfer and echoed back in webhook payloads.

response_schema.description: 201 Created. Standard Flutterwave envelope with the full transfer object.

id.description: Transfer ID — use with /transfers/{id} for retrieval, update and retry.

recipient.description: Polymorphic recipient (bank | mobile_money | wallet | cash_pickup | crypto).

gotchas: Flutterwave v4 uses OAuth2 client credentials. POST client_id and client_secret as form-encoded body to https://idp.flutterwave.com/realms/flutterwave/protocol/openid-connect/token to obtain an access_token (valid 10 minutes), then use it as Bearer in the Authorization header. The FLUTTERWAVE_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS env var stores base64(client_id:client_secret).

POST /transfers requires recipient_id (and sender_id for cross-border) pre-created via POST /transfers/recipients and POST /transfers/senders. Use POST /direct-transfers (orchestrator) if you want to inline sender/recipient data without pre-creating them.

payment_instruction.amount.applies_to controls whether amount.value is denominated in destination_currency or source_currency — pick deliberately on cross-currency corridors because Flutterwave will FX the other side using its quoted rate.
Always send X-Trace-Id (12-255 chars, required) and X-Idempotency-Key (12-255 chars, optional but strongly recommended) so retries are safe and the request is correlated in Flutterwave's logs.

When action is "scheduled", disburse_option (date_time YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS + timezone) becomes required. When action is "deferred", the transfer is created in NEW/PENDING but not disbursed until PUT /transfers/{id} with initiate=true is called.
Reference is optional but if supplied must be 6-42 alphanumeric+hyphen characters and unique across all transactions — reusing returns 409 RESOURCE_CONFLICT.
Response status values are UPPERCASE (NEW, PENDING, FAILED, SUCCESSFUL, CANCELLED, INITIATED). Do not lowercase before comparison.
